author: hamanasu_ruka
content: Oh hey maybe this is the right place to talk about ethereum-privacy projects on Github like `tor-js`? I'd like to help get hidden services working in it.

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: I feel like criminal liability for open source developers has been obviously in the works since PGP, moreso since Bittorrent

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: The US relaxing export controls on cryptographic software was a practical move, not any kind of moral shift

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: Just like the legalization of drugs, gambling, and even the repeal of prohibition has always been solely about tax revenue and never an acknowledgement that the government had no right to regulate those things in the first place

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: And of course Bitcoin has done as well as it has because Wall Street decided to get into the game. Meanwhile people get prosecuted for trading outside the regulated system.

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: For cash at least: it's always the choke points they go after.

author: greystroke
content: Unfortunately DOJ is trying to weaken the liability protections in the clarity act negotiations

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: My dad has been saying for as long as I can remember that eventually all interesting software development would have to be anonymous, and any software that wasn't corpo-controlled would be illegal.

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: The US will not only weaken protections for its own developers but force those changes onto other countries through trade agreements. And that's a bipartisan practice.

author: hamanasu_ruka
content: The parties just use slightly different justifications because of the slight differences in their particular billionaire donors
